description: Backfilling or updating data inside a compressed/cold chunk, causing failures or expensive decompression.
body: Failure mode: a late or backfilled write targets a chunk already compressed for cold storage, which either errors or forces a costly decompress-recompress cycle. Detection signals: write errors on old timestamps; sudden CPU spikes from decompression; compression policy age below the lateness window. Do not compress chunks that still receive writes. Safer fallback: set the compression age beyond the maximum lateness window, and batch genuine backfills into an explicit decompress→write→recompress maintenance step.
